summary:
Iter56 — Contracts branded PDF + client email endpoints. Backend: 11/11 pytest passed (test_contracts_iter56.py) covering PDF for existing + freshly created contract (pdfplumber verified all required tokens: 'Enterprise Q3 2026', 'PJX-2026', '§ 1 Term', '§ 2 Pricing', 'SERVICE PROVIDER', 'CLIENT', 'Contract Value', 'USD 48,000', 'Projexino Solutions Pvt Ltd', 'Page 1 of', '18' tax), route-ordering (GET /contracts/{cid} still returns JSON not PDF), AI draft email in all 3 tones (warm/brief/professional — all returned

-wrapped body and PJX-…/for-your-review subject), email validation (empty to → 400, empty subject → 400), Gmail-disconnected → 400 (NOT 500) with 'Gmail' + '/app/settings' hint, email log endpoint {items,total}, and Company Profile GET (letterhead singleton reused). Also ran HR letters regression: 20/20 pass across iter53+54+55. Frontend Playwright: login → contracts config for d11920aac3… → both new header buttons `ctr-btn-download-pdf` (ghost) and `ctr-btn-email-client` (orange) appear BEFORE Suspend/Renew/Cancel (verified DOM order: dlIdx=143 < emIdx=148 < Suspend=152 < Renew=157 < Cancel=163). PDF download via page.expect_download → 90,097 bytes, %PDF header ✓. Email modal prefill ✓ (subject 'PJX-2026-199C86 — Enterprise Q3 2026 for your review', include-pdf checkbox checked, body template). Xino AI draft (tone=warm) → body changed from 324 → 582 chars (real Gemini output). Preview toggle: textarea↔preview swap works. Send validation toasts fire ('Please add at least one recipient in \'To\'', 'Subject is required'). Gmail-disconnected: backend correctly returns HTTP 400, modal stays open ✓, BUT frontend toast shows generic 'AxiosError: Request failed with status code 400' instead of the friendly 400-detail (Gmail credentials invalid — reconnect from /app/settings). Close (X) button dismisses modal.

minor.issue: Nested `pricing` sub-object in create payload is ignored — created contracts default to USD/0. Users must PATCH pricing afterwards (matches Section 2 UI flow, but is not obvious to API consumers). Documented workaround in test.

ui_bugs.issue: On send failure, toast surfaces raw AxiosError text ('AxiosError: Request failed with status code 400') instead of the backend's friendly detail message ('Gmail credentials invalid — please reconnect from /app/settings'). Extract `err?.response?.data?.detail || err.message` in the catch block.

action_items: In EmailContractModal.send() catch block, replace generic err.message toast with `err?.response?.data?.detail || err.message` so users see the friendly Gmail-disconnected hint pointing to /app/settings.
(Optional) Document that POST /api/contracts intentionally ignores nested `pricing` — consumers must PATCH after create. Alternatively, allow pricing in create body for API-first users.
(Pre-existing critical review, from iter55) Backend has no server-side validation of `signature_data_url` — this is a security gap for HR letters; unrelated to iter56 but still outstanding.
critical_code_review_comments: contracts_module.py `email_contract` catches Gmail refresh failure and returns 400 (good). However `service.users().messages().send(...)` is still wrapped only in a generic Exception → 502; specific Google API errors (auth revoked mid-request, quota) all collapse to 502 with truncated string. Consider inspecting HttpError.resp.status and mapping 401 → 400 for consistency.

AI draft endpoint does not check `client.email` and uses `client.get('primary_contact') or client.get('contact_name')` — screenshot showed AI addressed 'Hi Naeema' correctly because `naeema` was captured. Fine for now.

`_render_contract_pdf` calls WeasyPrint synchronously inside an async handler. For a 90 KB PDF this is <100 ms so acceptable, but under load it will block the event loop. Consider `run_in_executor` for isolation.
Frontend downloadPdf() correctly uses responseType:'blob' + ObjectURL. Missing revokeObjectURL cleanup — a small memory leak per download session. Add URL.revokeObjectURL(url) after the click.

EmailContractModal — the toast for send-error only exposes err.message. When the backend returns 400 with `detail`, that friendly hint is lost. This is the single blocking action item — see frontend_issues.

The default HTML body template is inserted into a plain <textarea> and re-parsed as HTML on send — safe because backend accepts raw HTML, but a naive user pasting `<script>` will send it. Consider a lightweight sanitize step server-side before Gmail send.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (12/13 Playwright checks — only the toast-detail issue is a real bug; modal stays open + backend 400 verified via network capture)

seed_data_creation: Test class TestContractPdf.test_pdf_from_fresh_contract creates a temp client (TEST_iter56_pdfclient) + contract, PATCHes pricing, verifies PDF, then deletes both in finally block. All ephemeral — no residual data. Verified via /api/clients listing after run: only original naeema client remains.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter56 adds branded contract PDF (WeasyPrint, ~90 KB) + client-email drawer to the Contracts module. Shared letterhead singleton (`hr_letter_settings.singleton`) now powers BOTH HR letters and Contracts — do not break that unification. Endpoints: GET /api/contracts/{cid}/pdf, POST /api/contracts/{cid}/ai-draft-email, POST /api/contracts/{cid}/email, GET /api/contracts/{cid}/emails. Frontend testids: ctr-btn-download-pdf, ctr-btn-email-client, ctr-email-modal, ctr-email-{to,cc,subject,tone,ai-draft,body,preview,toggle-preview,include-pdf,send,close}. Playwright helper: use `page.expect_download()` for PDF download testing; use immediate polling for sonner toasts because they auto-dismiss ≤4s. Gmail is disconnected in this env — email send WILL fail with 400 (not 500). The action item for main agent is a 1-line frontend fix to surface `err.response.data.detail` in the send-error toast. Backend testing quirk: POST /api/contracts ignores nested `pricing` — PATCH after create to set currency/value (I documented this pattern in the test file).
rca of the issue: N/A — feature verification. Single minor frontend UX bug identified: EmailContractModal.send() catch block uses `err.message` (which axios sets to 'Request failed with status code 400') instead of unwrapping `err.response.data.detail`. Backend correctly emits the friendly Gmail hint per the try/except in contracts_module.py:800-803 — verified via network capture (200ms after click: 400 response). Fix is ~2 LOC in the modal's send handler.
